text: Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust, Shirdi, is the Governing and Administrative body of the Shree Saibaba's Samadhi Temple and all others temples on this premise, and devoted towards teachings of Saibaba “Sabka Malik Ek” and providing amenities to Sai devotees who are visiting Shirdi and Sai Samadhi Temple.  Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust, Shirdi, is the authorized body to control and manage the day-to-day activities at the Shree Saibaba Samadhi Temple.
It also provides various facilities like Accommodation, Meals (Free), refreshments, and lot more.

The Sansthan Trust also runs School and Colleges (Junior and Senior), English Medium School from class Jr KG to Std X, Industrial Training Institute (ITI), Drinking Water Supply, Hospitals (Shree Saibaba Superspecility Hospital and Shree Sainath Hospital on Charity basis).

 Shirdi, a small rural town in the Rahata Taluka of Ahilyanagar district in the Maharashtra state of India. Shirdi is a secular place where all religions are treated as one & a belief in the power of Faith & Patience is foremost. A place where all heads bow down in prayer, where faith prevails, where hopes are built, where patience pays, and where infinite joy and everlasting contentment abound. Such is the glory of the place which belongs to the Holy Saint, a true repository of wisdom, who pleased all with pious equality and gifted mankind ornaments of humanity and peace by saying "
The footprints of Saibaba have made this town a holy place.
Millions of devotees continuously flock here from all over India and abroad.
 Shirdi is where the holy soul of
- affectionately known as “
” rests today.

Shree Saibaba appeared at Shirdi in his human incarnation. For 60 years Baba served mankind and preached his precious teachings to the world from here, and then went for contemplation (Samadhi) in Shirdi itself.

The footprints and the deeds of Saibaba have turned this small town into a unique holy place for people of all castes, creeds and religions. Saibaba delivered the universal slogan of "Shraddha - Saburi", i.e.
Faith and Patience for everyone, from here itself.
The place of his 'Samadhi' has become a center-point for innumerable devotees, infusing them with the joy of life.
While visiting Shirdi, one experiences complete peace of mind, a strong self-confidence, and a great sense of purpose.
During his contemplation (Samadhi) services, Shree Saibaba had consoled his devotees with these words - "After I take Samadhi, my bones will speak from the grave, and people will throng here."
His message is being experienced till today.
Shirdi is a convenient place to visit throughout the year, during all seasons.
Baba's Shirdi is one place where even today all his devotees come with empty hands, but filled with Hope, and go back "Blessed with Contentment with an Everlasting Smile on their faces".

 The Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust in Shirdi is currently overseen by an Ad-Hoc Committee appointed by the Honourable Bombay High Court, Sambhajinagar Bench.  This Ad-Hoc Committee is responsible for the management and administration of the Trust, ensuring its operations align with legal and ethical standards. The committee's formation underscores the importance of judicial oversight in the governance of significant religious and charitable institutions.

 Donation in between 10000 to 24999 will have the privileges of either one free VIP Aarti or one free VIP Darshan for a maximum of 5 devotees, along with 5 Udi packets and 1 Laddu.  Donation in between 25000 to 50000 will have the privileges of either one free VIP Aarti and one free VIP Darshan or Two free VIP Aarti’s or Two free VIP Darshan’s for a maximum of 5 devotees, along with 5 Udi packets, 2 Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1 Shree Satcharitra Book.  Donation in between 50001 to 99999 will have the privileges of either Two free VVIP Aarti’s or Two free VVIP Darshan’s for a maximum of 5 devotees, along with 5 Udi packets and 2 Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1 Shree Satcharitra Book.  Donation in between 100000 to 999999 will have the special privileges.

For VVIP Darshan, the donor gets 2 free passes in the first year. After that, 1 pass is added every year, and this continues until the number of years reaches the donation amount divided by 1 lakh (for example: if the donation is ₹3,00,000, then 3 years). The same rule applies for VVIP Aarti—2 free passes in the first year, and then 1 extra pass each year up to the number of years equal to donation ÷ 1 lakh. For VIP Darshan, the donor will get 1 free pass every year for a lifetime. For VIP Aarti, the passes depend on the donation amount (example: ₹1 lakh = 1 pass, ₹3 lakhs = 3 passes) and these passes can be used for a lifetime. For Pooja, the donor gets 1 free lifetime pass, which can be used anytime.
Along with 5 Udi packets and 3 Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1 Shree Satcharitra Book, 1 3D Desk note holder, 1 Sanman Shawl, and 1 Abhishek / Satyanarayana Coupon.

 Donation in between 10 Lakhs to 50 Lakhs will have the following privileges For VVIP Darshan, the donor receives 2 passes in the first year, and from the following year onward, 1 extra pass is added every year for a lifetime. For VVIP Aarti, the number of first ‑ year passes is calculated by dividing the donation amount by ₹10 lakhs and then multiplying the result by 2 (for example, a ₹20 ‑ lakh donation gives 4 passes because 20 ÷ 10 = 2, and 2 × 2 = 4). From the next year onward, 1 pass will be added every year for a lifetime. For Pooja, the donor gets 1 lifetime pass that can be used anytime. For Cloth Offering, the donor receives 1 pass, and its validity in years depends on the donation amount—specifically, the number of valid years equals the multiple of every ₹10 lakhs donated (for example, ₹30 lakhs = 3 years of validity).

 Shree Sai Satyavrata Puja According to Chapter 13 of Shree Sai Satcharita, a devotee named Bhimaji Patil from Narayangaon was suffering from a chronic illness.
Despite undergoing various medical treatments and religious rituals, he did not find relief from his ailment.
Eventually, he sought refuge in Shree Saibaba based on the advice of Nanasaheb Chandorkar. With the blessings and grace of Saibaba, his illness was cured.
Bhimaji Patil was a devotee of Lord Narayana (Lord Vishnu), and after experiencing the healing powers of Saibaba, he realized that Baba is the incarnation of the Supreme God, Shree Narayana.

This led to the initiation of a new tradition of worship called "Shree Sai Satyavrata Puja." As a result, the worship of Saibaba's idol is now performed during the Satyanarayana Puja to fulfil the desires of Sai devotees. In the concluding chapters of the Satyanarayana Puja, Bhimaji Patil provides a brief description of his experience.  Here are some instructions regarding “Shree Sai Satyavrata Pooja”: o

The KIOSK machines installed by Sansthan are available at the Shree Sai Baba Temple in Shirdi is a self-service facility designed to simplify the process of obtaining VIP Darshan Passes and VIP meal tickets for devotees. These machines provide a user-friendly, queue-free experience, allowing visitors to generate their passes independently without having to stand in manual ticket counters.

The primary objective of the kiosk machines is to make the VIP Darshan pass and VIP meal ticket booking faster, more convenient, and accessible to all devotees visiting the temple. With tens of thousands of people visiting Shirdi daily, these kiosks help reduce congestion and waiting time.
Devotee Details Entry Devotees start by entering their basic details on the kiosk screen.
The interface is simple and intuitive, ensuring that even first-time users can handle the process easily.
Payment Through UPI QR Only Once the details are submitted, the kiosk generates a UPI QR code for payment. ✅ UPI QR is the only accepted payment method at these kiosks.
Debit cards, credit cards, and cash payments are not supported.
Receipt Generation After successful UPI payment, the kiosk automatically prints a VIP Darshan pass / VIP meal ticket receipt, which the devotee can use at the temple’s designated entry gate.

Which Online facilities can be availed on this website? Devotees can avail following online facilities:  Darshan  Aarti  Room Booking  Donation  Saileela Membership  Life Membership  Books 2. Who can avail online facilities? Any devotee after successful registration can avail online facilities.
What is the procedure for availing online facilities?
o First, register yourself with us.
o Sign in with your Log in details.
o Select your facilities.
o Make online payment through Credit/Debit card or Internet Banking.
o After successful payment, your facilities will be booked.

How can I make successful Registration? o Click on the Register tab / click on https://online.sai.org.in/#/registration o Fill your details like Email id, Name, Contact details, photo (max 1MB), valid photo id. o Please add the email id sai.support@sai.org.in in your address book.

o You will get confirmation email on your registered Email id. o Activate your account by clicking on the activation link or copying the link into your browser and clicking it within 24 hours of your registration.
o OTP verification process sent to the registered mobile number also to be completed for successful registration.
How can I confirm whether my facilities are booked or not?
o After successful payment, you will be directed to acknowledgment page. You can print the acknowledgement. You will also receive order confirmation mail, which will contain acknowledgement.
What Should I do, if I do not get an order confirmation mail?
o You can also take print out of acknowledgement from Transaction History.
o Check whether you have added email id sai.support@sai.org.in in your address book.
o Check your spam mails for verification.
Check the transaction status on your screen. If status is failed then you have to make payment again. Check your bank account, if money is debited from your account.

If not, please make another attempt of payment. o If money is debited from your account and still you have not received the acknowledgement, drop a mail to customer support sai.support@sai.org.in with the details like:  Name  Address  Mobile Number  Registered Email Id 7.

What is the meaning of colours given to the dates in the calendar? Dates having green colour are available for booking. Dates with grey colour means quota is not released for booking or the dates have been already passed. Dates with red colour means quota is full for those dates.
How early and how late can I book a room online before my arrival date in Shirdi? You can book a room up to 60 days in advance, including for the current day, subject to availability.

After booking the room, can I change Check-in and Check-Out dates? Once a room is booked, you cannot change the check-in/check-out date. If you want to postpone your check in time, you can call helpdesk and request for postponement of check in time. However, check in time cannot be extended beyond check out time.
How many open transactions are allowed for online room booking? In one login account, only 1 open transaction is allowed.
Only after you have availed the existing booking, you may proceed for another booking.

Can I change my accommodation room type after I have booked the room? No, you cannot change the accommodation details after the booking has been made. In case you wish to do so, you can cancel the existing booking and proceed with a new booking. Appropriate cancellation charges will apply as per the Terms & Conditions.
While booking accommodation, I am getting the error message "No Search results found".
What does that mean?
If an error message "No Search Results found" appears while booking the accommodation rooms, it means that no rooms are available for the search criteria given by you.

How check out date is calculated? Your reservation date is 21st May 2024, with check-in time slot selected as 08:00 AM- 11:59 AM & number of days as 1/2 day(s). Then the check-out date & time will be 24hrs/48hrs from check in date & time slot. For 1 day check-out date & time is 22nd May 2024 & 8:00 AM. For 2 days check-out date and time is 23rd May 2024 & 8:00 AM.

You have to report to the local counter in Shirdi.
